title: Managing Your Knowledge Base for Maximum AI Quality
description: Best practices for building and maintaining a high-quality Knowledge Base that maximizes AI assistant accuracy and usefulness.
body:
# Managing Your Knowledge Base for Maximum AI Quality

## Why Knowledge Base Quality Matters

The Eunoia AI Assistant is only as good as the documents in your Knowledge Base. The assistant retrieves information using semantic similarity — it finds the documents most relevant to a question and generates an answer from them. Unclear, incomplete, or outdated documents lead to incomplete or inaccurate answers.

A well-maintained Knowledge Base is the single most important factor in AI assistant quality.

## What to Upload

### High-Priority Content (Upload First)
- **FAQs**: The most common questions guests and staff ask, with clear answers
- **Policies**: Check-in/check-out, cancellation, pet policy, damage policy, payment terms
- **Service descriptions**: Room types, amenities, dining options, spa services
- **Standard procedures**: Check-in process, complaint handling, emergency procedures
- **Price lists and packages**: Current rates, seasonal offers, group booking terms

### Medium-Priority Content (Upload Second)
- Staff training materials and onboarding guides
- Supplier and vendor information
- Local area information (nearby restaurants, attractions, transport)
- Brand guidelines and communication standards
- Escalation procedures and contact lists

### Lower-Priority Content (Upload as Available)
- Historical reports and performance reviews
- Meeting notes and internal memos
- Archived documents and superseded policies

## Writing Content That Works Well with AI

### Use Clear, Direct Language
The AI works best with clear, unambiguous writing. Avoid:
- Vague terms like "as applicable" or "subject to conditions" without explaining the conditions
- Jargon that only long-serving staff would recognize
- Abbreviations without defining them first

### Be Complete Within Each Document
The AI reads documents independently. If a document references another policy, also include the key points of that policy (or upload the referenced policy separately). Do not rely on the reader knowing context from other sources.

### Use Descriptive Titles
Document titles are important for retrieval. A title like "Late Check-Out Policy — Maximum Extension Hours and Fees" is far more useful than "Policy Doc 3" or "LCO".

### Structure with Headings
Use headings (H1, H2, H3) to break content into clear sections. The AI chunks documents into passages for retrieval. Well-structured content with headings produces better chunks and more accurate retrieval.

### Include Examples and Scenarios
Where possible, include examples of how policies apply in real situations:
- "If a guest arrives before 2:00 PM check-in time and the room is available, offer early check-in complimentary."
- "If no rooms are available, offer complimentary luggage storage and inform them you will notify them by text when the room is ready."

## Keeping Content Up to Date

### Create a Review Schedule
Set calendar reminders to review your Knowledge Base documents at regular intervals:
- **Monthly**: Pricing, availability, seasonal promotions
- **Quarterly**: Operating procedures, service descriptions
- **Annually**: Full Knowledge Base audit

### Update Before Going Live
Before rolling out the AI assistant to new staff or guests, ensure the most important documents are current. Outdated policies in the Knowledge Base lead to confident but wrong answers.

### Archive, Do Not Delete
When a policy changes, update the document rather than deleting the old one. If you must remove a document, check whether the AI's answers change noticeably and whether those changes are improvements.

## Organizing Your Knowledge Base

### Category Structure
Organize documents into logical categories:
- Guest-Facing: Policies and information guests need to know
- Staff Operations: Internal procedures and guidelines
- Training: Onboarding and professional development
- Services: Detailed service descriptions and packages

### Avoid Duplication
Duplicate content causes confusion — the AI may retrieve contradictory versions of the same information. If the same policy appears in multiple documents, consolidate them into one authoritative source.

## Measuring Knowledge Base Effectiveness

Ask these questions regularly:
1. When staff use the assistant, do they get useful answers?
2. Are there common questions the assistant cannot answer? (Add documents on those topics.)
3. Are there answers that seem outdated? (Review and update those documents.)
4. Do staff trust the answers they receive? (If not, audit the source documents.)

## Getting Help

If you need guidance on structuring your Knowledge Base or improving AI response quality, contact the Eunoia support team at support@eunoia.ai.
